title: Embedding-Space Cross-Table Predictability Trajectories: Standard vs PRMP HeteroGNN on Amazon Reviews
summary: Interactive notebook demonstrating Predictive Residual Message Passing (PRMP) on heterogeneous graph neural networks for Amazon Video Games review-rating prediction. Constructs a heterogeneous graph (reviews ↔ products ↔ customers), trains Standard vs PRMP HeteroSAGE models in pure PyTorch, and tracks per-layer embedding-space predictability (Ridge R², Random Forest R², Mutual Information) across training epochs for each FK link. Shows that PRMP filters predictable cross-table information, achieving lower embedding R² on product→review links while improving task MAE. Mini dataset contains 10K sampled reviews with product/customer aggregated features and aligned parent-child matrices from the McAuley Amazon Video Games corpus.
